nodes.name: Data Ingest
nodes.tagline: Raw market data captured from scraper API
nodes.logic: Fetches one record per ticker per pipeline run at $0.0015/record. Tracks null fields automatically. Feeds the null triage node with a completeness score (0–100). This is the entry point — without it, nothing runs.
nodes.failure_mode: Hard abort — no data means no signal. Rate limits are handled with exponential backoff.
nodes.insight: Every pipeline run starts here. At 12 trades/day on the $2K tier, this node fires ~252 times per trading day.

nodes.name: Null Triage
nodes.tagline: Detects missing fields and routes to cheapest filler
nodes.logic: Scans the ingested record for null fields. Categorises each missing field by which provider can fill it most cheaply. Builds a priority-ordered enrichment queue — Finnhub free first, then FMP, then Alpha Vantage, then Claude AI as last resort. Avoids paying for data you already have.
nodes.failure_mode: Non-blocking. If triage fails, enrichers are called in default order. Minor cost inefficiency.
nodes.insight: Futures contracts (e.g. GC=F Gold) legitimately have many null fields. Triage prevents wasted API calls on fields that are structurally unavailable.

nodes.name: Multi-Provider Enrichers
nodes.tagline: Parallel null-filling waterfall across paid data providers
nodes.logic: Three enrichment providers run in parallel after triage. Finnhub fills analyst ratings and insider transactions. FMP fills fundamentals, earnings estimates, and ratios. Alpha Vantage fills OHLCV bars and technical indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, EMA, ATR, ADX). Results are deep-merged into a unified enriched record. Fields already present are never overwritten.
nodes.failure_mode: Partial tolerance. If one provider fails, others continue. The enriched record is marked with completeness_pct and which fields remain null.
nodes.insight: At the $200 tier, only Finnhub free + FMP Starter + AV free are active. At $2K tier, paid plans unlock higher rate limits and more endpoints. This node contributes most to signal completeness.

nodes.name: L2 Order Book Depth
nodes.tagline: Real-time bid/ask ladder and microstructure signals
nodes.logic: Captures the top 10 bid and ask levels every 500ms. Computes the order book imbalance ratio: (bid_vol − ask_vol) / (bid_vol + ask_vol). A positive imbalance suggests buy pressure; negative suggests selling. Imbalance is fed into the confidence scoring node with a 20% weight on volume confirmation. Also detects thin books that could cause high slippage on execution.
nodes.failure_mode: Graceful degrade. If L2 is unavailable, confidence node receives volume_confirmation = 0. Gate threshold may not be met, resulting in skip.
nodes.insight: Only available on Polygon paid tier. At the MVP $200 tier, this node is inactive — the pipeline runs L1 only, which is a major reason win rate is capped at 60% at that tier.

nodes.name: Options Greeks Node
nodes.tagline: Delta, Gamma, Theta, Vega — live volatility surface
nodes.logic: Fetches options Greeks for the at-the-money contract nearest expiry. IV rank and IV percentile are the primary signals used downstream — high IV rank (>70) indicates elevated options pricing relative to history, which can flag earnings catalyst or event-driven trade setups. Delta is used to estimate directional sensitivity. Feeds into the sentiment and confidence nodes.
nodes.failure_mode: Non-blocking. If ORATS is unavailable, Greeks are nulled and confidence node runs without IV context. Confidence ceiling drops slightly.
nodes.insight: Active only from the $2K institutional tier onward. Unlocking this node is one of the biggest qualitative jumps in signal quality — IV rank alone adds several percentage points to win rate accuracy.

nodes.name: Regime Detection AI
nodes.tagline: Classifies current market state every 15 minutes
nodes.logic: Claude analyses ADX value, VIX level, market breadth indicator, and 20-day rolling volatility to classify the market into one of 7 states: trending_bull, trending_bear, mean_reverting, high_volatility, low_volatility, crisis, or undefined. Each regime maps to a recommended strategy playbook (momentum, mean_reversion, hold, defensive). The active playbook adjusts position sizing, stop-loss widths, and confidence gate thresholds dynamically.
nodes.failure_mode: Falls back to last known regime classification. If stale by >30 min, strategy defaults to 'hold' until re-classified.
nodes.insight: This is the intelligence layer that stops the system from running momentum strategies during a mean-reverting regime and vice versa. Without regime detection, the win rate degrades by ~3–5% across all tiers.

nodes.name: AI Sentiment Scorer
nodes.tagline: News + analyst signals → bullish/bearish score with reasoning
nodes.logic: The most token-intensive node. Claude receives: latest news headlines (last 4h), analyst rating actions (upgrades/downgrades), insider transaction summary, and earnings surprise history. It outputs a sentiment score (−1 to +1), a label (bullish/bearish/neutral), a risk flag (low/medium/high/critical), and a black_swan_detected boolean. The risk_flag triggers circuit-breaker logic. Reasoning is stored verbatim for audit.
nodes.failure_mode: If Claude API is unavailable, sentiment is set to neutral (0.0) and confidence drops. The confidence gate becomes harder to pass, reducing trade frequency but protecting capital.
nodes.insight: Contributes 20% weight to the confidence gate. The black_swan_detected flag has fired on major earnings misses and macro announcements — it's the system's early-warning layer. At the $200 tier, only basic headline text is passed; at $2K+, full structured data is provided.

nodes.name: Correlation Guard
providers: Internal — reads from PostgreSQL correlation_matrix table
nodes.logic: Computes rolling 20-day pairwise Pearson correlations across all instruments in the watchlist. If the candidate trade instrument has |correlation| > 0.70 with any currently open position, the correlation_guard score is penalised. High correlation clustering (e.g. holding 3 positions that all move together) is flagged and feeds into confidence scoring with a 10% weight. Acts as a diversification enforcement layer.
nodes.failure_mode: If correlation data is stale (>8h), the guard score defaults to 0.5 (neutral penalty). Threshold enforcement continues at a reduced weight.
nodes.insight: Critical for multi-position portfolios. A fund running 5 concurrent long equity positions that are all 0.85 correlated is essentially concentrated in one bet. This node prevents that silently.

nodes.name: Confidence Gate
nodes.tagline: 72% composite threshold — the trade filter that protects capital
providers: Internal — aggregates all upstream node outputs
nodes.logic: Weighted composite of 6 factors: technical_alignment (25%), volume_confirmation (20%), ai_sentiment (20%), risk_reward_ratio (15%), regime_filter (10%), correlation_guard (10%). If composite_score ≥ 0.72, the trade passes and an action (buy/sell) with direction (long/short) is emitted. Stop-loss and take-profit levels are calculated using ATR-14 and the active regime playbook. Signals below 0.72 are logged as 'skip' — they do not execute.
nodes.failure_mode: If any upstream node fails, that factor defaults to its neutral/penalty value. The gate continues to operate — it never crashes. It may simply reject more trades on bad data days.
nodes.insight: This is the most important node. At the MVP tier, fewer nodes feed it so composite scores are less informed. At the $2K institutional tier, all 6 factors are fully populated, which is why win rate climbs from 60% to 68%.

nodes.name: RL Execution Node
nodes.tagline: Reinforcement learning selects optimal order type and timing
nodes.logic: After the confidence gate passes, this node decides whether to execute as a market order, limit order, or stop-limit. It analyses real-time L2 book spread, current volatility (ATR), and time-of-day liquidity patterns to minimise slippage. Market orders are used only when the spread is tight (<5bps) and liquidity is deep. Limit orders are preferred for illiquid instruments. All execution decisions are sent to IBKR TWS via the REST API. Fills are logged with actual vs intended price for ongoing RL training.
nodes.failure_mode: If IBKR connection fails, the order is queued for retry (up to 3 attempts). If IBKR TWS is offline, the circuit breaker is triggered immediately.
nodes.insight: Slippage is the silent killer of algorithmic strategies. This node reduces average slippage from 15bps (market order default) to 8bps at the $2K tier, directly improving net P&L. Only active when confidence gate passes.

nodes.name: Circuit Breaker
nodes.tagline: Autonomous kill switch — halts all trading on anomaly detection
nodes.logic: Monitors 9 trigger conditions in real time: daily_loss_limit (2% per instrument), portfolio_var_breach (5% VaR), ai_anomaly (black_swan_detected flag from sentiment node), fat_finger_detected (order size >5× normal), flash_crash (price movement >3% in <60s), api_data_glitch (completeness_pct <40%), correlation_breach, manual_override, drawdown_limit. When triggered, it cancels all open IBKR orders and halts new signal processing until manually resolved by an operator.
nodes.failure_mode: The circuit breaker is the last line of defence. It has no failure mode — it is designed to fail safe (halt all activity) rather than fail open.
nodes.insight: Has been triggered by: stale data feeds, pre-market gap-up events, and earnings black swan moments. In all cases, it prevented trades that would have caused outsized losses. This node runs on every single pipeline cycle regardless of what else is happening.

importance_rank.name: Data Ingest
importance_rank.reason: Entry point — nothing runs without it

importance_rank.name: Confidence Gate
importance_rank.reason: The filter that directly controls win rate

importance_rank.name: AI Sentiment
importance_rank.reason: 20% of confidence score, detects black swans

importance_rank.name: Regime Detection
importance_rank.reason: Prevents strategy mismatch in wrong market regime

importance_rank.name: Enrichers
importance_rank.reason: Fills the data gaps that drive signal quality

importance_rank.name: L2 Order Depth
importance_rank.reason: Microstructure signals for entries and slippage
